semantic.description: A function \(F(x)\) is a primitive of \(f(x)\) on an interval \([a, b]\) if \(F\) is differentiable throughout \([a, b]\) and \(F'(x) = f(x)\) for all \(x \in [a, b]\).
The indefinite integral of a function \(f(x)\) is the set of all its primitives, expressed as \(F(x) + c\) for \(c \in \mathbb{R}\), and denoted \(\int f(x)\, dx = F(x) + c\).
